text.title: database-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for database-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: God table: a single table with 50+ columns for loosely related entities.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Makes every query wide; most columns are NULL for any given row; adding indexes for different query patterns bloats write overhead. Normalize into focused tables and join when needed.

failure-mode.description: Using FLOAT or DOUBLE for monetary amounts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: IEEE 754 floating-point cannot represent most decimal fractions exactly. Repeated arithmetic accumulates rounding errors. Always use NUMERIC(12,2) or BIGINT cents for money.

failure-mode.description: Applying ON DELETE CASCADE to foreign keys without careful review of the deletion chain.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A cascade chain 3+ tables deep means a single parent delete wipes all descendant rows. A developer deleting a test user can wipe all their orders, payments, and shipments in a single query.
